ref: While Abraham was still alive, he gave gifts to the sons of Hagar and Keturah. He also sent their sons to live in the east far from his son Isaac, and when Abraham died, he left everything to Isaac. The Death of Abraham
text: Ama e ka daa wɔ ŋkpa to na e daŋ kɛ mo jipocheana be mbia na asɔ nna pɔɛŋ nseŋ n shin nɛ b lar Aizek kutɔ n ya ka wɔ epeŋipetekpa.
translated: My mother told me that my father had gone to the farm and would return in the evening with a goat that he had bought from the market and that I should prepare the stew for him.

ref: Then Jacob prayed: You, Lord, are the God who was worshiped by my grandfather Abraham and by my father Isaac. You told me to return home to my family, and you promised to be with me and make me successful.
text: Kumo be kaman nɛ Jeekɔb kule Ebɔrɛ ŋ kaŋɛ: “N tuto Eebraham nɛ Aizek be Ebɔrɛ, n Nyɛnpe, fo e kaŋɛ ma fanɛ m bɛta n yɔ ma efuli so nɛ ma basa pe nɛ fo shin nɛ ŋ ki asɔwura.
translated: Then he said to Jacob, “I am God, the God of your father, Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Isaac. I will give to you and your descendants this land on which you are lying.

ref: Right after Isaac had given Jacob his blessing and Jacob had gone, Esau came back from hunting.
text: Aizek ka baŋ nɛfa Jeekɔb n loge nɛ e baa lar mo kutɔ nɛ mo da Isɔɔ malɛ shi mbe kekpaŋ na to m ba kaa luri.
translated: Isaac blessed Jacob and not Esau because his eyes had grown dim and he could no longer see well.

ref: People from all over the world came to Egypt to buy grain, because the famine was so severe in their countries.
text: Basa daa shi efuli kama so nna gba a ba Josɛf kutɔ m ba kaa tɔ ayu, ŋkpal manɛ so, akoŋ na daa shi ga nna ashi kaplɛkama.
translated: He was loved by God and was full of wisdom, and God was with him.

ref: and named the light “Day” and the darkness “Night.” Evening came, then morning—that was the first day. The Second Day
text: E ka wora loŋ na nɛ e nase kefulto be saŋɛ na kapa nseŋ nase tentembiri be saŋɛ na malɛ kanyɛ. Ndoŋ nna nɛ kanyɛ biri nɛ kare chɛ ŋ ki kachɛ lelemu juŋkparso na.
translated: The dog chases the goat and the goat runs to the farm and climbs on top of the heap of cow dung.

ref: Some years later God decided to test Abraham, so he spoke to him. Abraham answered, “Here I am, Lord.”
text: Eebraham wɔtɔ loŋ nɛ kachako Ebɔrɛ ba nɛ e ba wora mo ŋ keni, nseŋ tre mo: “Eebraham!” Nɛ Eebraham shuli ŋ kaŋɛ: “Ma nde.”
translated: Then Abraham called his servant, the oldest of his household, who was in charge of all his possessions, and said to him: “Abraham!” And his servant answered: “Here I am.”

ref: Rebekah still had no children. So Isaac asked the Lord to let her have a child, and the Lord answered his prayer.
text: Aizek nɛ Rebɛka ka ta abar Rebɛka daa maa kurge nɛ Aizek kule Enyɛnpe Ebɔrɛ na n sa mo nɛ Enyɛnpe Ebɔrɛ na nu mbe kabɔrɛkule na n shin nɛ Rebɛka di kedampo.
translated: Isaac and Rebecca went to consult the oracle. Rebecca was pregnant and Isaac was troubled about her because she was going to give birth. So they went to consult the oracle about her and the oracle said to him Rebecca your wife will give birth to two children.

ref: So I am ready to make an agreement with you, and we will pile up some large rocks here to remind us of the agreement.”
text: Amoso shin nɛ ma nɛ fo e nase kɔnɔ n sa abar nɛ k baa la shɛda be kusɔ a wɔ anyi be kefɛato.”
translated: “We do not want to hear talk of war; we want peace, so let us all come together to deliberate on how to achieve it.”

ref: The Lord said to Cain: What's wrong with you? Why do you look so angry?
text: Ndoŋ nna nɛ Enyɛnpe Ebɔrɛ na bishi Keen le: “Manɛ e ba nɛ fo nya agbo n tiŋ to loŋ?
translated: What did Enyɛnpe Ebɔrɛ say to Keen: “Why did you go and bring this matter to my compound?

ref: he saw Benjamin and told the servant in charge of his house, “Take these men to my house. Slaughter an animal and cook it, so they can eat with me at noon.”
text: Josɛf ka wu Bɛnjamin nɛ mo daana na nɛ e kaŋɛ mbe laŋ to be nyɛrbi be enimu le: “Keta basa ere n yɔ ma pe n ya mɔ kusɔbɔya n wora ajibi nɛ ma nɛ bumo e ji kapa ere.”
translated: Joseph saw that Benjamin's brother was with them and said to the steward of his house, "Put my silver cup in the sack of the youngest one, along with his grain money."

ref: Joseph was served at a table by himself, and his brothers were served at another. The Egyptians sat at yet another table, because Egyptians felt it was disgusting to eat with Hebrews.
text: B daŋ ta Josɛf be ajibi nna n yili mo kakpa ko nseŋ ta mo daana na malɛ n chena kakpa ko nseŋ ta Ijipt be basa nɛ b daa wɔ ndoŋ na peya n yili kakpa ko. B daŋ wora loŋ nna, ŋkpal manɛ so, k daa la ekishi nna fanɛ Ijipt be esa e tu Hibruw be esa n chena a ji.
translated: They went with Joseph to Egypt and sold him to the Ishmaelite merchants who were on their way to Egypt. They sold him for twenty shekels of silver and took him to Egypt.

ref: Then on the seventeenth day of the seventh month of the year, the boat came to rest somewhere in the Ararat mountains.
text: Kufɔl shunusopo to be kachɛ kuduashunusopo nɛ kuloŋgboŋ na ya yili kebee nɛ baa tre Ararat na so.
translated: The flood that destroyed the people was the one that also destroyed Kuduashunusopo and Kuloŋgboŋ, and they were the ones who went to Mount Ararat.

ref: years to buy your sheep and goats. During that time you kept changing my wages.
text: Menyi alɛ kikɛ nyi kananɛ m bugi kagbene n shuŋ men tuto.
translated: The elders assembled and said that they would not allow the stranger to stay.

ref: he said: These people are working together because they all speak the same language. This is just the beginning. Soon they will be able to do anything they want.
text: nseŋ kaŋɛ le: “Ŋkpal basa ere ka la basa koŋwule a gbar ŋgbar koŋwule nseŋ fara a wora kusɔ nɛ baa wora ere, kumo ere kusɔ kama nɛ baa fɛ kewora, baaŋ tiŋ n wora kumo.
translated: The elders said: “The chief’s elders are not like the commoners, for the chief’s elders are the strong pillars that hold up the commoners, and the commoners are like the ropes that hold up the elders, you cannot do without the other.”

ref: and asked, “Hagar, where have you come from, and where are you going?” She answered, “I'm running away from Sarai, my owner.”
text: nseŋ bishi mo: “Heega, Sɛɛrai be kebita, nnɛ nɛ fo shi m ba nfe nɛ nnɛ malɛ nɛ fee yɔ?” Nɛ e kaŋɛ: “Mee shile ashi n chepe Sɛɛrai kutɔ nna.”
translated: The chief said: “Friend, Sɛɛrai has been seized, what do you say we go and see or do you want to go and pay the fine?” He replied: “I don’t have money to pay the fine for Sɛɛrai.”

ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.
text: Eebram daa la damawura nna kashenteŋto nseŋ daa kɔ mbolpɔ nɛ mboe nɛ ana nɛ shuwa nɛ gbiti.
translated: The elders assembled and discussed the matter but they could not find a solution to it and it was left unsettled.

ref: Let my share be the food that my men have eaten. But Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre went with me, so give them their share of what we brought back.
text: Ŋ gbagba maaŋ ta fo shɛŋ ama kusɔ nɛ mbrantiɛ na ji kena na to na nawule. Ama shin nɛ n teriana Anɛr nɛ Eshkɔl nɛ Mamre e ta asɔ nɛ an bar na be ako.”
translated: “I will make a covenant with you, and I will multiply your seed after you, as I covenanted with Abraham, and with Isaac, and with Jacob; and I will give you a land for an heritage, the land of Canaan, as an everlasting possession.”

ref: The smell of the burning offering pleased the Lord, and he said: Never again will I punish the earth for the sinful things its people do. All of them have evil thoughts from the time they are young, but I will never destroy everything that breathes, as I did this time.
text: Enyɛnpe na ka nu sarga na be efɛa belbelso na nɛ e kaŋɛ mbe kumu le: “N nyi fanɛ dimɛdi be kebia to kikɛ mbe nfɛra fɛso lubi, ama m maaŋ naŋ kpal dimɛdi so n sho kasawule ere kɔnɔ kikɛ. Ma alɛ maaŋ naŋ mur asɔ futeso fanɛ kananɛ n wora ere kikɛ.
translated: My mother used to tell us that when we were still small, she would prepare a special porridge for us and then she would tell us to go and sell it in the market, but she warned us to be careful with the money we made from selling it.

ref: “That isn't so!” Joseph insisted. “You've come here to find out where our country is weak.”
text: Nɛ Josɛf kaŋɛ bumo: “Ayai, mannɛ aloŋ nna, men ba nna nɛ men ba dara m pin efuli ere so be kakpa nɛ bekumpo maŋ wɔ.”
translated: And Joseph said to his brothers: “Behold, I have had a dream, and I see that we are binding sheaves in the field and my sheaf is standing and your sheaves are bowing down to mine.”

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Ama fo alɛ kaŋɛ fanɛ feeŋ shin nɛ ŋ ki asɔwura nseŋ shin nɛ ma kanaŋ e salga fanɛ teku ase be leele nɛ esa maaŋ tiŋ ŋ karga na.”
translated: My mother used to say that if you see a python on the farm, you should not kill it, because it protects the farm from pests and other destroyers.
